paragraphs: Sometimes your trip may involve arriving at one New York airport and departing from another. For example, you might fly into LaGuardia (LGA) from St. Paul, Minnesota, and then depart from JFK on an international flight.

blocks.h2: Transportation options when traveling between airports
paragraphs: For traveling between JFK and EWR:
For traveling between JFK and LGA:
For more details on fares and schedules, consult LIRR opens in a new tab and NJ Transit opens in a new tab.
Taxis offer reliable transportation between airports, and you will find them waiting outside each terminal—just follow the signs.
Taxis between JFK and LGA Estimated travel time: 30-60 minutes Estimated fare: $60
Taxis between JFK and EWR Estimated travel time: 60-100 minutes Estimated fare: $160
Trips may be subject to additional tolls and surcharges above standard metered fare depending on time of day, route traveled, and other factors.
You can use Uber, Lyft, and other ride apps to connect between airports. Trips may be subject to additional tolls and surcharges depending on time of day, route traveled, and other factors
You can pre-arrange transportation between airports with a shuttle service, which may be best if you are traveling with a group or have oversized or large quantities of luggage.
Shuttles between JFK and LGA Estimated travel time: 30-60 minutes
Shuttles between JFK and EWR Estimated travel time: 60-100 minutes
Shuttle service options: ETS Air Shuttle: +1 (718)-221-5341 Airlink New York: +1 (212)-812-9000
You may also choose to arrange private transfers via limousine or van service, which can be arranged in advance.
